name: Bugsnag Error Monitoring API
what_it_does: Bugsnag monitors application stability by capturing unhandled errors, crashes, and ANRs — provides a Data Access API for querying error trends, releases, and project stability metrics from agent workflows.
use_cases: Reading error trends and stability metrics for automated deployment health agents
Querying recent errors by release version to trigger rollback decisions in CI/CD
Integrating Bugsnag error data into incident management and on-call agent workflows
Monitoring error rate trends and anomaly detection from agent-driven alerting systems
Pulling release stability scores for engineering performance dashboards
not_for: Browser-only error tracking (use Sentry for richer web platform support)
Log aggregation and search (use Datadog or Axiom for general logging)
High-frequency event ingestion beyond error notifications
best_when: Your mobile (iOS/Android) or web applications already use Bugsnag and you need to query error data, stability scores, or release metrics programmatically.
avoid_when: You need log management, APM, or tracing in addition to error tracking — Sentry offers a broader developer-facing observability platform.

auth.notes: Personal Auth Token in Authorization header. Organization-level and user-level tokens. No granular scopes — token provides access to all projects the user can access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Team $59/month; Business $139/month; Enterprise custom. Data API access on all paid plans.
pricing.notes: Error event volume determines pricing. Data Access API is included. Mobile crash reporting has separate pricing considerations.

performance.rate_limits: 300 requests per minute; some endpoints have lower per-endpoint limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST errors with errors array. Rate limit errors not always well-documented. Data API documentation is behind the Apiary docs site which is dated. Webhook payloads are well-structured.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All Data API operations are read-only GET requests — fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Organization slug required in every URL path — not obvious from Account settings where to find it
Data Access API returns aggregate metrics, not individual error events — use webhooks for real-time individual errors
Error grouping IDs are unstable — Bugsnag re-groups errors over time, old error_id references may point to merged groups
Stability scores are calculated asynchronously — newly deployed releases take time to show accurate scores
API documentation is on Apiary (third-party) and may lag the actual API behavior

security_score_components.security_notes: Single-scope API token with no granular permissions. GDPR compliant with EU data option. SOC 2 Type II. IP allowlisting available on Enterprise. No OAuth — simple token auth.
